title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: The Essential Chat Support plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all...
description: The Essential Chat Support plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 1.0.1. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to reset all plugin configuration settings — including general settings, display rules, custom CSS, and WooCommerce tab settings — to their defaults by sending a POST request with ecs_reset_settings=1.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8681.

title: Crypt::DSA versions through 1.19 for Perl use 2-args open, allowing existing files to be modified.
description: Crypt::DSA versions through 1.19 for Perl use 2-args open, allowing existing files to be modified.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The use of 2-args open in Crypt::DSA versions through 1.19 for Perl introduces a vulnerability that could allow attackers to modify existing files, potentially leading to data integrity issues, unauthorized file changes, or privilege escalation if exploited.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Crypt::DSA that addresses the vulnerability, or switch to an alternative library if no patch is available.
Audit and monitor file access logs for any unauthorized modifications to detect potential exploitation.
Restrict permissions on sensitive files and directories to minimize the impact of this vulnerability.

title:
Crypt::DSA versions before 1.20 for Perl generate seeds using rand.

Seeds were generated using...
description:
Crypt::DSA versions before 1.20 for Perl generate seeds using rand.

Seeds were generated using Perl's built-in rand function, which is predictable and unsuitable for security usage.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The use of Perl's built-in rand function for seed generation in Crypt::DSA versions before 1.20 makes cryptographic operations vulnerable to prediction, potentially allowing attackers to compromise digital signatures or other cryptographic processes relying on DSA.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Crypt::DSA version 1.20 or later to ensure secure seed generation using a cryptographically strong random number generator.
Audit systems and applications using Crypt::DSA to identify potential vulnerabilities and implement patches or mitigations.
Monitor for any signs of exploitation or compromise related to DSA-based cryptographic operations and rotate keys if necessary.

title: radare2 6.1.5 contains a use-after-free vulnerability in the gdbr_pids_list() function within the...
description: radare2 6.1.5 contains a use-after-free vulnerability in the gdbr_pids_list() function within the GDB client core that allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service or potentially execute arbitrary code by sending malformed thread information responses. Attackers can trigger the vulnerability by causing qsThreadInfo to fail after qfThreadInfo successfully allocates RDebugPid structures, resulting in double-free memory corruption when the error path attempts to clean up the list.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The use-after-free vulnerability in radare2 6.1.5's gdbr_pids_list() function could allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to system compromise or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately update radare2 to the latest patched version if available or apply any security fixes provided by the vendor.
Restrict access to systems running radare2 and monitor for unusual activity, especially related to GDB client interactions.
Implement memory corruption detection tools and enable runtime protections to mitigate exploitation attempts.

title: ORSEE (Online Recruitment System for Economic Experiments) 3.1.0 contains an authenticated Remote...
description: ORSEE (Online Recruitment System for Economic Experiments) 3.1.0 contains an authenticated Remote Code Execution vulnerability in the participant profile field processing subsystem. Certain field configurations accept values beginning with the prefix "func:" which are passed directly into an eval() call inside tagsets/participant.php and tagsets/options.php.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary code on the server by exploiting the eval() function in the participant profile field processing subsystem. This could lead to full system compromise, data exfiltration, or further attacks on connected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately update ORSEE to a patched version if available, or apply any available security patches addressing this vulnerability.
Restrict access to the ORSEE system to trusted users only and monitor for unusual activity, particularly in participant profile field configurations.
Conduct a security audit of the system to identify and mitigate any unauthorized changes or potential backdoors introduced via this vulnerability.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to enforce the PostEditTimeLimit...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to enforce the PostEditTimeLimit on non-message post fields which allows an authenticated user to modify post file attachments, props, and pin status after the edit window has expired via the post patch and update API endpoints.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00631

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4053.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 Fail to validate the...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 Fail to validate the response body of proxied images, which allows a remote attacker to enact client-side DoS via an SVG file served from an attacker-controlled origin under a non-SVG Content-Type header (e.g. image/png) embedded in an og:image meta tag or Markdown image link.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00630

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4054.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in Utils::parseUrl()...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in Utils::parseUrl() that allows authenticated users to inject JavaScript via malformed URLs in comments. Attackers can craft URLs with unescaped quotes to inject event handlers, stealing admin session cookies and achieving full application takeover when visitors view affected FAQ pages.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46367.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an information disclosure vulnerability in the getIdFromSolutionId...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an information disclosure vulnerability in the getIdFromSolutionId() method that lacks permission filtering, allowing unauthenticated attackers to enumerate restricted FAQ entries and read their titles via the /solution_id_{id}.html endpoint. Attackers can sequentially iterate solution IDs to discover all FAQs including those restricted to specific users or groups, leaking sensitive metadata through redirect Location headers and page canonical links.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46366.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in search.twig where...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in search.twig where result.question and result.answerPreview are rendered with the raw filter, disabling autoescape protection. Attackers with FAQ editor privileges can inject HTML-entity-encoded payloads that bypass html_entity_decode(strip_tags()) processing in SearchController.php, executing arbitrary JavaScript in every visitor's browser context including administrators.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46361.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in AbstractAdministrationController::userHasPermission() that fails to terminate execution after sending a forbidden response. Attackers can access all permission-protected admin pages by requesting their URLs as authenticated users, exposing admin logs, user data, system information, and application configuration.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to bypass authorization checks and access sensitive admin pages, potentially exposing critical data such as admin logs, user information, system configurations, and application settings. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized modifications, or further exploitation of the system.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to phpMyFAQ version 4.1.2 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Review access logs for suspicious activity and unauthorized access attempts to admin pages.
Implement additional access control mechanisms, such as IP whitelisting or two-factor authentication, for admin accounts.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a missing authorization vulnerability in the DELETE /admin/api...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a missing authorization vulnerability in the DELETE /admin/api/content/tags/{tagId} endpoint that allows any authenticated user to delete tags. Any logged-in user, including regular frontend users, can delete arbitrary tags by sending a DELETE request with a valid session cookie, resulting in permanent data loss and disruption of FAQ organization.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows any authenticated user, including low-privilege frontend users, to delete tags in the FAQ system. This can lead to permanent data loss, disruption of FAQ organization, and potential operational inefficiencies for organizations relying on phpMyFAQ for knowledge management.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to phpMyFAQ version 4.1.2 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected endpoint by implementing stricter role-based access controls (RBAC).
Monitor logs for any unauthorized DELETE requests to identify potential abuse and take corrective actions.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an unauthenticated SQL injection vulnerability in BuiltinCaptcha:...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an unauthenticated SQL injection vulnerability in BuiltinCaptcha::garbageCollector() and BuiltinCaptcha::saveCaptcha() methods that interpolate unsanitized User-Agent headers into DELETE and INSERT queries. Unauthenticated attackers can exploit the public GET /api/captcha endpoint by crafting malicious User-Agent headers to perform time-based blind SQL injection, extracting sensitive data including user credentials, admin tokens, and SMTP credentials from the datab

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46364.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a sql injection vulnerability in CurrentUser::setTokenData that...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a sql injection vulnerability in CurrentUser::setTokenData that allows authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL by injecting malicious OAuth token claims. Attackers with Azure AD accounts containing SQL metacharacters in display names or JWT claims can break out of string literals and execute arbitrary database queries.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 allows authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL queries, potentially leading to data breaches, unauthorized data manipulation, or full database compromise. This could result in exposure of sensitive information, disruption of services, and reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to phpMyFAQ version 4.1.2 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Review and sanitize OAuth token claims and input data to prevent injection attacks.
Conduct a security audit of database access logs to identify any signs of exploitation.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an improper restriction of excessive authentication attempts...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an improper restriction of excessive authentication attempts vulnerability in the /admin/check endpoint, which accepts arbitrary user-id parameters without session binding or rate limiting. Unauthenticated attackers can brute-force any user's six-digit TOTP code by submitting POST requests with sequential token values, bypassing two-factor authentication to gain full administrative access.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-45010.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in SvgSanitizer:...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in SvgSanitizer::decodeAllEntities() that limits recursive entity decoding to 5 iterations, allowing attackers to bypass sanitization. Authenticated users with FAQ_EDIT permission can upload malicious SVG files with deeply nested ampersand encoding around numeric HTML entities to reconstruct javascript: URLs, which execute arbitrary JavaScript when clicked by other users viewing the uploaded SVG.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users with specific permissions to upload malicious SVG files that can execute arbitrary JavaScript. This could lead to session hijacking, data theft, or unauthorized actions within the application, impacting the confidentiality and integrity of the system.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to phpMyFAQ version 4.1.2 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict FAQ_EDIT permissions to only trusted and essential users to limit exposure.
Implement additional input validation and sanitization for uploaded SVG files at the application level.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an insufficient authorization vulnerability in admin-api routes...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an insufficient authorization vulnerability in admin-api routes that allows authenticated ordinary users to access administrative endpoints by only checking login status instead of verifying backend privileges. Attackers with valid frontend user accounts can access sensitive backend operational information including dashboard versions, LDAP configuration, Elasticsearch statistics, and health-check data.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-45009.

title: Schlix CMS 2.2.6-6 contains a remote code execution vulnerability that allows authenticated...
description: Schlix CMS 2.2.6-6 contains a remote code execution vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code by uploading malicious extension packages through the block manager. Attackers can upload a crafted ZIP file containing PHP code in the packageinfo.inc file and trigger execution by accessing the About tab of the installed extension.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47964.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a path traversal vulnerability in Client::deleteClientFolder that...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a path traversal vulnerability in Client::deleteClientFolder that allows admins with INSTANCE_DELETE permission to delete arbitrary directories. Attackers can submit traversal sequences like https://../../../<path> in the client URL parameter to recursively delete directories outside the intended clientFolder scope.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-45008.

title: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in FAQ creation and...
description: phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in FAQ creation and update endpoints that bypass sanitization through encode-decode cycles. The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with FAQ_ADD permission to inject malicious script tags via question or answer parameters, which execute in every visitor's browser when FAQ content is rendered with the raw Twig filter.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46363.

title: CVE-2026-42174 - Kirby is an open-source content management system. Prior to versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0, user avatar cr
description: Kirby is an open-source content management system. Prior to versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0, user avatar creation, replacement and deletion are not gated by user update permissions. This issue has been patched in versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized users to create, replace, or delete user avatars without proper permissions. This could lead to unauthorized content manipulation, potential phishing attacks, or reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Kirby versions 4.9.0 or 5.4.0 immediately to patch the vulnerability.
Review user permissions and access controls to ensure proper restrictions are in place.
Monitor logs for any unauthorized avatar changes to identify potential exploitation.

title: CVE-2026-42137 - Kirby is an open-source content management system. Prior to versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0, `pages.access/
description: Kirby is an open-source content management system. Prior to versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0, `pages.access/list` and `files.access/list` permissions are not consistently checked in the Panel and REST API. This issue has been patched in versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Kirby CMS allows unauthorized access to sensitive page and file data due to inconsistent permission checks in the Panel and REST API. This could lead to data exposure or unauthorized modifications.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Kirby CMS to the patched versions 4.9.0 or 5.4.0 immediately.
Review and audit access permissions for sensitive pages and files to ensure proper restrictions are in place.
Monitor logs for any unusual or unauthorized access attempts to the Panel or REST API.

title: CVE-2026-42069 - Kirby is an open-source content management system. Prior to versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0, read access to
description: Kirby is an open-source content management system. Prior to versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0, read access to site, user and role information is not gated by permissions. This issue has been patched in versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42069.

title: CVE-2026-42051 - Kirby is an open-source content management system. Prior to versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0, the system API
description: Kirby is an open-source content management system. Prior to versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0, the system API endpoint leaks license data and installed version to authenticated users. This issue has been patched in versions 4.9.0 and 5.4.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42051.

title: CVE-2026-41311 - LiquidJS is a Shopify / GitHub Pages compatible template engine in pure JavaScript. Prior to version
description: LiquidJS is a Shopify / GitHub Pages compatible template engine in pure JavaScript. Prior to version 10.25.7, a circular block reference in {% layout %} / {% block %} causes an infinite recursive loop, consuming all available memory (~4GB) and crashing the Node.js process with FATAL ERROR: JavaScript heap out of memory. This allows any user who can submit a Liquid template to perform a Denial of Service attack. This issue has been patched in version 10.25.7.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41311.

title: CVE-2026-41163 - bubblewrap is a low-level unprivileged sandboxing tool. From version 0.11.0 to before version 0.11.2
description: bubblewrap is a low-level unprivileged sandboxing tool. From version 0.11.0 to before version 0.11.2, if bubblewrap is installed in setuid mode then the user can use ptrace to attach to bubblewrap and control the unprivileged part of the sandbox setup phase. This allows the attacker to arbitrarily use the privileged operations, and in particular the "overlay mount" operation, allowing the creation of overlay mounts which is otherwise not allowed in the setuid version of bubblewrap. This issue has been patched in version 0.11.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41163.

title: CVE-2026-8207 - Gibbon versions before v30.0.01 are affected by an authenticated SQL Injection vulnerability by abus
description: Gibbon versions before v30.0.01 are affected by an authenticated SQL Injection vulnerability by abusing the  Tracking/graphing https://github.com/GibbonEdu/core/blob/c431e25fdc874adece5d2dc7e408e9aa2d1abadb/modules/Tracking/graphing.php#L145  feature. Successful exploitation requires Teacher or higher privileges. Exploitation could result in unintended read/write activities to the underlying database.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8207.

title: CVE-2026-7652 - The LatePoint plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Account Takeover via Weak Password Recovery Mech
description: The LatePoint plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Account Takeover via Weak Password Recovery Mechanism in the unauthenticated guest booking flow in versions up to, and including, 5.5.0 This is due to the save_connected_wordpress_user() function propagating a LatePoint customer's email address to its linked WordPress user account via wp_update_user() without any ownership verification, combined with the guest booking flow's ability to overwrite an existing customer's email through phone-based merge without authentication. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to overwrite the email address of a non-super-admin WordPress user account that is not yet linked to a LatePoint customer, enabling full account takeover by subsequently triggering the standard WordPress password-reset flow to the attacker-controlled address granted the plugin is configured with WordPress user integration enabled, phone-based contact merging, and customer authentication disabled. Administrator accounts on single-site installs are not affected.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7652.

title: CVE-2026-6667 - PgBouncer before 1.25.2 did not perform an appropriate authorization check for the KILL_CLIENT admin
description: PgBouncer before 1.25.2 did not perform an appropriate authorization check for the KILL_CLIENT admin command. All users with access to the administration console (which itself requires authorization) could run this command. It would have been correct to allow only users listed in the admin_users parameter.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6667.

title: CVE-2026-6666 - A possible null pointer reference in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 could lead to a crash, if a server send
description: A possible null pointer reference in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 could lead to a crash, if a server sends an error response without SQLSTATE field.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6666.

title: CVE-2026-6665 - The SCRAM code in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 did not check the return value of strlcat() correctly when
description: The SCRAM code in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 did not check the return value of strlcat() correctly when building the contents of the SCRAM client-final-message. A malicious backend that sends a SCRAM server-final-message with a long nonce can trigger a stack overflow.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6665.

title: CVE-2026-6664 - An integer overflow in network packet parsing code in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 bypasses a boundary ch
description: An integer overflow in network packet parsing code in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 bypasses a boundary check and can lead to a crash. An unauthenticated remote attacker can crash PgBouncer with a malformed SCRAM authentication packet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6664.

title: CVE-2026-41705 - Spring AI's MilvusVectorStore#doDelete(List) implementation is vulnerable to filter-expression injec
description:
Spring AI's MilvusVectorStore#doDelete(List) implementation is vulnerable to filter-expression injection via unsanitized document IDs.
Spring AI 1.0.x: affected from 1.0.0 through latest 1.0.x; upgrade to 1.0.7 or greater. Spring AI 1.1.x: affected from 1.1.0 through latest 1.1.x; upgrade to 1.1.6 or greater.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41705.

title: CVE-2026-44313 - Linkwarden is a self-hosted, open-source collaborative bookmark manager to collect, organize and arc
description: Linkwarden is a self-hosted, open-source collaborative bookmark manager to collect, organize and archive webpages. Prior to version 2.13.0, a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in the fetchTitleAndHeaders function allows authenticated users to make arbitrary HTTP requests to internal services due to insufficient URL validation that only checks for "http://" or "https://" prefixes. This issue has been patched in version 2.13.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44313.

title: CVE-2026-42455 - Linkwarden is a self-hosted, open-source collaborative bookmark manager to collect, organize and arc
description: Linkwarden is a self-hosted, open-source collaborative bookmark manager to collect, organize and archive webpages. In versions 2.14.0 and prior, the archive upload endpoint (POST /api/v1/archives/[linkId]?format=4) accepts HTML files (text/html) without sanitizing JavaScript content. When the archive is later accessed via GET /api/v1/archives/[linkId]?format=4, the HTML is served with Content-Type: text/html from the Linkwarden origin, without any Content-Security-Policy header. This allows arbitrary JavaScript execution in the context of the authenticated Linkwarden sessio. At time of publication, there are no publicly available patches.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42455.
